December 21,2000
Nancy A. Space, Deputy County Counsel
San Benito County
Office of the County Counsel
481 Fourth Street
Hollister, CA 95023
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 00-263
Dear Ms. Space:
Please find enclosed the materials discussed in our telephone conversation. These
materials include:
l) Romney Advice Letter, No. A-99-263
2) Tyrrell Advice Letter, No. I-99-171
3) Draft amendments to Regulation 18708 (strike-out version and clean copy)
The Romney letter, on page 6, provides inforrnation on the "legally required
participation" rule. The Tyrrell lener addresses decisions by members of the Prop.10 -
commission in Napa County. Of particular note is page 20 of this letter which explains
segmentation as it relates to funding decisions by that commission. Additionally, you
will find enclosed the draft of amended Regulation 18708 regarding disclosures required
under the "legally required participation" rule.I
I hope these materials will be helpful to you in gathering additional information
regarding the contemplated process of the commission as it carries out its funding
allocation. Specifically, to answer your questions, we require facts regarding the funding
decisions before the commission, the evaluative methods to be used in selecting
I We anticipate these amendments to be effective February I , 2001 . Your name has been added to
the mailing list of interested persons who will receive ltnal approved versions of the regulations amended at
the FPPC's December 8, 2000 meeting.


F
File No. 00-263
Page No. 2
recipients (i.e., steps where the commissioners are expected to exercise their discretion),
the identity of applicants who are economic interests of any of the commissioners, and
any proposed manner of implementing the "legally required participation" rule and./or
proposed manner of segmenting decisions in which one or more commissioners has a
conflict of interest. Please submit this information to us by January 26,2001. If we do
not hear from you by that date, we will consider your request for advice withdrawn.
For your convenience, I have enclosed the FPPC document entitled "Can I Vote?
Conflict of Interest Overview" which may be helpful to the commissioners in
understanding the conflict of interest rules. If you have any questions regarding the
enclosed materials or any of the matters we have discussed, please do not hesitate to call
me at (916) 322-7771.
Sincerely,

sections.question_synthetic: Whether San Benito County's Children and Families Commission complies with conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The request for advice was withdrawn by the requestor on January 30, 2001, and no final conclusion was issued.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two related letters. The December 21 letter is an incomplete FPPC response requesting additional information before issuing advice. The January 30 letter is a withdrawal acknowledgment with no formal Q/A sections. No substantive conclusion was reached due to withdrawal.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether San Benito County's Children and Families Commission complies with conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The request for advice was withdrawn by the requestor on January 30, 2001, and no final conclusion was issued.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal of an FPPC advice request. The first letter (December 21, 2000) requested additional factual information about San Benito County's Children and Families Commission's funding decisions and conflict of interest implementation procedures, with a deadline of January 26, 2001. The second letter (January 30, 2001) confirms that the requestor withdrew the advice request.
